mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0% and remains elevated for 2+ months, the restrictive monetary stance increases borrowing costs and reduces equity valuations through higher discount rates. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days as market participants price in slower growth and margin compression.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 5.25% indicates restrictive cycle
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with elevated rates
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS > 5.0% sustained, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer purchasing power. A month-over-month increase in UNRATE of 0.3 percentage points or greater triggers a negative equity return signal over 60 trading days as forward earnings expectations decline and recession risk rises.
evidence_refs: fact:Current UNRATE at 4.5% provides baseline for deterioration detection
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite unemployment rise, or correlation between UNRATE changes and forward returns remains below 0.15 in absolute value over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) growth decelerates below 2% year-over-year while simultaneously housing starts (HOUST) contract, signaling weakening consumer demand and construction activity. This joint deterioration in demand indicators predicts negative 20-day SPX returns as forward GDP growth expectations are revised downward.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS YoY growth < 2% AND HOUST < 1400k units
evidence_refs: fact:Current RSAFS at 373946M and HOUST at 1474k units near trigger threshold
regime_hint: demand-driven slowdown with synchronized weakness across consumer and construction
failure_condition: Joint signal produces positive 20-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect (measured as coefficient on RSAFS×HOUST interaction term) is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines month-over-month while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated above 3.5% year-over-year, creating stagflationary pressure. This combination signals reduced real economic capacity and persistent cost pressures, producing negative 60-day SPX returns as profit margins compress and growth stalls.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO MoM decline AND CPIAUCSL YoY > 3.5%
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 112.69 and CPIAUCSL at 207.39 establish baseline for stagflation detection
regime_hint: stagflationary environment with supply constraints and persistent inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 55% of stagflation-triggered instances, or the stagflation interaction term (INDPRO decline × CPIAUCSL elevation) shows Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth accelerates above 200k jobs per month, signaling robust labor market strength and supporting consumer confidence. This always-on trend mechanism predicts positive 5-day SPX returns as strong employment data reduces recession fears and supports equity risk appetite.
evidence_refs: fact:Current PAYEMS at 138038k provides baseline for growth measurement
regime_hint: any regime with positive employment momentum
failure_condition: Positive payroll growth months produce negative 5-day SPX returns in > 50% of instances, or correlation between PAYEMS MoM changes and 5-day forward returns is below 0.10 in absolute value over 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerates above 2.5% annualized rate, indicating robust economic expansion. In expansionary regimes, this always-on signal predicts positive 60-day SPX returns as higher growth supports corporate earnings and equity valuations expand on improved economic outlook.
evidence_refs: fact:Current GDPC1 at 11532.8B provides baseline for growth acceleration detection
regime_hint: post-shock recovery or sustained expansion phase
failure_condition: Quarters with GDPC1 growth > 2.5% annualized produce negative 60-day forward returns in > 45% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate declines by 0.5 percentage points or more over a 3-month period while inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains above 2.5%, the Fed is easing into persistent inflation. This regime-conditional signal predicts positive 20-day SPX returns as monetary accommodation supports asset prices despite inflation concerns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines 0.5pp in 3 months AND CPIAUCSL YoY > 2.5%
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS at 5.25% and CPIAUCSL at 207.39 establish baseline for easing-with-inflation detection
regime_hint: monetary easing cycle with sticky inflation
failure_condition: Easing-with-inflation episodes produce negative 20-day returns in > 50% of instances, or the interaction coefficient (FEDFUNDS decline × CPIAUCSL elevation) is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) accelerates above 1.5% month-over-month while inflation (CPIAUCSL) decelerates below 2.5% year-over-year, signaling healthy supply-side growth without inflationary pressure. This regime-conditional signal predicts positive 5-day SPX returns as the market rewards non-inflationary growth and improved real economic capacity.
evidence_refs: fact:Current INDPRO at 112.69 and CPIAUCSL at 207.39 establish baseline for goldilocks supply scenario
regime_hint: non-inflationary growth regime with supply-side strength
failure_condition: Goldilocks supply episodes produce negative 5-day returns in > 55% of instances, or the interaction term (INDPRO acceleration × CPIAUCSL deceleration) shows Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.
